affordances.name: Evidence anchored compression check
affordances.mechanism: When reality is too noisy to reason about directly, require the answer to compress details into a usable pattern while naming the concrete evidence, operating constraint, or feedback that keeps the abstraction from floating free of reality.

use_when: Decision-makers need a usable pattern before they can evaluate trade-offs, priorities, or causal structure.
The case requires moving between concrete observations and a governing principle.
The recommendation must be portable, teachable, and fast to grasp without losing its core logic.
do_not_use_when: The abstract map is being treated as reality rather than a simplified representation.
The abstraction is accurate but does not help action, prediction, or decision-making.
The model is elegant but no longer anchored to evidence, constraints, or feedback.
case_evidence_needed: The essential pattern or governing principle.
The evidence or operational constraint that keeps the abstraction valid.
The action or decision the abstraction should improve.

treatment_requirements.description: Require the answer to state the abstraction, the concrete evidence it compresses, the boundary where it stops being true, and the action it makes clearer.
evidence_required: The action, trade-off, or decision the abstraction changes.
good_output_shape: Compresses noisy detail into a usable model.
Names the evidence that supports the compression.
States where the map is not the territory.
Connects the abstraction back to action.

diagnostic_questions: What concrete details are being compressed into this abstract model?
Where would this map stop matching the territory?
How does the abstraction change the decision or next action?
misuse_guards: Do not treat an elegant map as reality.
Do not use accurate abstraction that cannot guide action.
Do not let high-level labels hide untested assumptions.

source_evidence.source_quote: Most useful when reality is too noisy to reason about directly
Works when teams must move between detail and governing principle
Best when communication needs compression without losing the core logic

review_notes.normalization_note: Batch 11 outcome: strong_affordance_record. The source supports abstraction as useful compression that must be re-grounded in evidence and action.
dropped_material: Did not extract logic trees, Feynman Technique, or digital twins as separate affordances.
Kept map-vs-territory as the primary misuse boundary.
open_questions: PR49 should test whether this card helps packets compress complexity without producing floating labels.

affordances.name: Abductive hypothesis framing check
affordances.mechanism: When sparse observations must become a provisional explanation or solution hypothesis, require the answer to state the abstract hypothesis, the observations and assumptions behind it, and the next evidence needed to prove, disprove, or replace it.

use_when: Limited observations must be turned into the most plausible explanatory or solution hypothesis before focused analysis can begin.
A team needs an abstract early guess that directs data gathering and analysis rather than more open-ended fact collection.
Design or strategy work requires suppressing assumptions and distilling observations into a conceptual model that can be tested.
do_not_use_when: The provisional hypothesis is being treated as a confirmed category or final abstraction.
The abstraction hides untested assumptions or selects evidence only to confirm the early guess.
The case already has enough concrete evidence and only needs compression back into an actionable model.
case_evidence_needed: The assumptions that must hold for the hypothesis to be plausible.
The evidence that would prove, disprove, or refine the hypothesis.
The competing hypothesis or disconfirming observation to test.

treatment_requirements.description: Require the answer to turn sparse observations into a provisional abstraction, expose its assumptions, and specify the smallest useful evidence plan that can prove or disprove it.
evidence_required: The candidate abstract explanation or solution.
The assumptions behind the candidate abstraction.
The focused evidence-gathering or disconfirmation plan.
good_output_shape: Lists assumptions that must be true.
Defines the next proof, disproof, or refinement evidence.

diagnostic_questions: What limited observations are we trying to explain with this abstraction?
What would have to be true for this hypothesis to be the right abstraction?
What evidence would prove, disprove, or refine the hypothesis next?
misuse_guards: Do not treat an abductive hypothesis as a confirmed model.
Do not let high-level labels hide untested assumptions.
Do not gather more facts than needed to prove or disprove the hypothesis.

source_evidence.source_quote: use a limited set of observations to generate the most plausible explanation
This process starts with formulating a hypothesis—an abstract, early guess at the answer—which then dictates the structure of the data gathering and analysis.
requires designers to suppress assumptions and distill observations into conceptual models.
Gather only enough facts to prove or disprove a hypothesis—anything more is a waste of time and effort.

review_notes.normalization_note: PR85 targeted enrichment: the source supports abductive hypothesis framing as a separate transaction from evidence-anchored compression.
dropped_material: Did not promote logic trees, Feynman explanation, or AI digital twins as separate abstraction affordances.
Kept evidence-anchored compression in the existing affordance.
open_questions: Packet review should test whether this card helps the receiver form a provisional hypothesis without converting it into a confirmed abstraction.

absence_records.reason: The source says maps are not reality and abstraction becomes dangerous when it floats free of evidence, operational constraints, or feedback.

source_evidence.source_quote: They are not reality in any way

absence_records.reason: The source warns that an abstract idea can be accurate but useless if it does not help direct action or decision-making.

source_evidence.source_quote: An abstract idea can be accurate but useless if it does not help direct action or decision-making.
process of synthesizing and refining information to capture the essential characteristics of a system or idea while purposefully ignoring nonessential detail.

review_notes.normalization_note: Batch 11 source reading found strong reviewed depth for abstraction as evidence-anchored compression. PR85 adds a separate abductive hypothesis card for sparse-observation cases where the abstraction is an early guess that directs evidence gathering.
dropped_material: PR85 rejected logic trees, Feynman explanation, and AI digital twins as separate abstraction cards.
No runtime, prompt, lane, packet, deterministic pressure, deterministic option selection, or user-facing change is implied.
open_questions: Later packet review should test whether abstraction cards remain grouped by transaction: compression/regrounding versus hypothesis framing/testing.
